label: has part
description: a core relation that holds between a whole and its part

label: preceded by
description: x is preceded by y if and only if the time point at which y ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which x starts. Formally: x preceded by y iff ω(y) <= α(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

label: precedes
description: x precedes y if and only if the time point at which x ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: x precedes y iff ω(x) <= α(y), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

label: occurs in
description: b occurs_in c =def b is a process and c is a material entity or immaterial entity& there exists a spatiotemporal region r and b occupies_spatiotemporal_region r.& forall(t) if b exists_at t then c exists_at t & there exist spatial regions s and s’ where & b spatially_projects_onto s at t& c is occupies_spatial_region s’ at t& s is a proper_continuant_part_of s’ at t

label: contains process
description: [copied from inverse property 'occurs in'] b occurs_in c =def b is a process and c is a material entity or immaterial entity& there exists a spatiotemporal region r and b occupies_spatiotemporal_region r.& forall(t) if b exists_at t then c exists_at t & there exist spatial regions s and s’ where & b spatially_projects_onto s at t& c is occupies_spatial_region s’ at t& s is a proper_continuant_part_of s’ at t

label: activates
description: convert (something, such as a provitamin) into a biologically active derivative

label: actuates
description: cause to act in a particular way; motivate

label: associates
description: combine or join with other parts

label: binds to
description: to combine with, form a bond with, or be taken up by a chemical or chemical structure
example: "An enzyme is structured in such a way as to be able to bind with its substrate"

label: causes
description: make happen

label: constrains
description: severely restrict the scope, extent, or activity of

label: controls
description: determine the behaviour or supervise the running of

label: degrades
description: break down or deteriorate chemically

label: destroys
description: end the existence of something by damaging or attacking it

label: directs
description: control the operations of; manage or govern

label: downregulates
description: lower the rate or level of (a process such as gene expression, or sensitivity to a physiologically active substance) by downregulation

label: enhances
description: intensify, increase, or further improve the quality

label: facilitates
description: make (an action or process) easy or easier

label: forces
description: make something happen

label: hinders
description: make it difficult for something to happen

label: inactivates
description: make inactive or inoperative

label: increases
description: become or make greater in size, amount, or degree

label: induces
description: bring about or give rise to
example: none of these measures induced a change of policy

label: inhibits
description: hinder, restrain, or prevent (an action or process)

label: initiates
description: cause (a process or action) to begin
example: he proposes to initiate discussions on planning procedures

label: limits
description: curtail or reduce in quantity or extent

label: mentions
description: refer to something

label: overproduces
description: produce an excessive amount
example: if soils are too fertile, the vines can overproduce, leading to vegetal aromas and flavors

label: phosphorylates
description: introduce a phosphate group into (a molecule or compound)

label: produces
description: cause (a particular result or situation) to happen or exist

label: provokes
description: call forth an action

label: reduces
description: diminish in size, amount, extent, or number

label: repress
description: inactivate (a gene or formation of a gene product)

label: reverts
description: return to previous state

label: secretes
description: (of a cell, gland, or organ) produce and discharge (a substance)
example: insulin is secreted in response to rising levels of glucose in the blood

label: shuts
description: cause to cease or suspend an operation or activity

label: suppress
description: inhibit the genetic expression of

label: triggers
description: initiate or set off

label: upregulates
description: increase the rate or level of (a process such as gene expression, or sensitivity to a physiologically active substance) by upregulation

label: has characteristic
description: Inverse of characteristic_of

label: participates in
description: a relation between a continuant and a process, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

label: has participant
description: a relation between a process and a continuant, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

label: is concretized as
description: A relationship between a generically dependent continuant and a specifically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant also depends on that same independent continuant. A generically dependent continuant may be concretized as multiple specifically dependent continuants.

label: concretizes
description: A relationship between a specifically dependent continuant and a generically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant also depends on that same independent continuant. Multiple specifically dependent continuants can concretize the same generically dependent continuant.

label: has function
description: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a function, in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

label: has quality
description: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a quality, in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

label: has role
description: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a role, in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

label: has disposition
description: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a disposition, in which the disposition specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

label: derives from
description: a relation between two distinct material entities, the new entity and the old entity, in which the new entity begins to exist when the old entity ceases to exist, and the new entity inherits the significant portion of the matter of the old entity

label: derives into
description: a relation between two distinct material entities, the old entity and the new entity, in which the new entity begins to exist when the old entity ceases to exist, and the new entity inherits the significant portion of the matter of the old entity

label: has allergic trigger
description: A relation between a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host and a material entity, in which the material entity is not part of the host, and is considered harmless to non-allergic hosts, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

label: has autoimmune trigger
description: A relation between a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host and a material entity, in which the material entity is part of the host itself, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

label: located in
description: a relation between two independent continuants, the target and the location, in which the target is entirely within the location

label: has 2D boundary
description: a relation between a material entity and a 2D immaterial entity (the boundary), in which the boundary delimits the material entity

label: tracheates
description: The relationship that holds between a trachea or tracheole and an antomical structure that is contained in (and so provides an oxygen supply to).

label: bounding layer of
description:
X outer_layer_of Y iff:
. X :continuant that bearer_of some PATO:laminar
. X part_of Y
. exists Z :surface
. X has_boundary Z
. Z boundary_of Y

has_boundary: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002002
boundary_of: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002000

label: coincident with
description: A relation that holds between two linear structures that are approximately parallel to each other for their entire length and where either the two structures are adjacent to each other or one is part of the other.

label: cell expresses
description: A relation that applies between a cell(c) and a gene(g) , where the process of 'transcription, DNA templated (GO_0006351)' is occuring in in cell c and that process has input gene g.

label: regulates transport of
description: A relationship that holds between a  process that regulates a transport process and the entity transported by that process.

label: occurrent part of
description: A part of relation that applies only between occurrents.

label: has component process
description: w 'has process component' p if p and w are processes,  w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type.

label: has ligand
description: A relationship that holds between between a receptor and an chemical entity, typically a small molecule or peptide, that carries information between cells or compartments of a cell and which binds the receptor and regulates its effector function.

label: transports
description: Holds between p and c when p is a transport process or transporter activity and the outcome of this p is to move c from one location to another.

label: occurs across
description: A relationship between a process and a barrier, where the process occurs in a region spanning the barrier. For cellular processes the barrier is typically a membrane. Examples include transport across a membrane and membrane depolarization.

label: has effector activity
description: A 'has effector activity' B if A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674),  A 'has component activity' B and B is the effector (output function) of B.  Each compound function has only one effector activity.

label: has soma location
description: Relation between a neuron and a material anatomical entity that its soma is part of.

label: fasciculates with
description: relationship between a neuron and a neuron projection bundle (e.g.- tract or nerve bundle) that one or more of its projections travels through.

label: synapsed by
description: Relation between an anatomical structure (including cells) and a neuron that chemically synapses to it.

label: synapsed to
description: Relation between a neuron and an anatomical structure (including cells) that it chemically synapses to.

label: dendrite synapsed in
description: Relation between a neuron and some structure  (e.g.- a brain region) in which its dendrite receives synaptic input.

label: overlaps
description: x overlaps y if and only if there exists some z such that x has part z and z part of y

label: innervates
description:
Relation between a 'neuron projection bundle' and a region in which one or more of its component neuron projections either synapses to targets or receives synaptic input.
T innervates some R
Expands_to: T has_fasciculating_neuron_projection that synapse_in some R.

label: partially overlaps
description: x partially overlaps y iff there exists some z such that z is part of x and z is part of y, and it is also the case that neither x is part of y or y is part of x

label: shares ancestor with
description: two individual entities d1 and d2 stand in a shares_ancestor_with relation if and only if there exists some a such that d1 derived_by_descent_from a and d2 derived_by_descent_from a.

label: spatially disjoint from
description: A is spatially_disjoint_from B if and only if they have no parts in common

label: connected to
description: a is connected to b if and only if a and b are discrete structure, and there exists some connecting structure c, such that c connects a and b

label: connects
description: c connects a if and only if there exist some b such that a and b are similar parts of the same system, and c connects b, specifically, c connects a with b. When one structure connects two others it unites some aspect of the function or role they play within the system.

label: supplies
description: Relation between an arterial structure and another structure, where the arterial structure acts as a conduit channeling fluid, substance or energy.

label: drains
description: Relation between an collecting structure and another structure, where the collecting structure acts as a conduit channeling fluid, substance or energy away from the other structure.

label: has component
description: w 'has component' p if w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type.

label: has phenotype
description: A relationship that holds between a biological entity and a phenotype. Here a phenotype is construed broadly as any kind of quality of an organism part, a collection of these qualities, or a change in quality or qualities (e.g. abnormally increased temperature). The subject of this relationship can be an organism (where the organism has the phenotype, i.e. the qualities inhere in parts of this organism), a genomic entity such as a gene or genotype (if modifications of the gene or the genotype causes the phenotype), or a condition such as a disease (such that if the condition inheres in an organism, then the organism has the phenotype).

label: develops from
description: x develops from y if and only if either (a) x directly develops from y or (b) there exists some z such that x directly develops from z and z develops from y

label: develops into
description: inverse of develops from

label: has gene product
description: x has gene product y if and only if x is a gene (SO:0000704) that participates in some gene expression process (GO:0010467) where the output of that process is either y or something that is ribosomally translated from y

label: expressed in
description: x expressed in y if and only if there is a gene expression process (GO:0010467) that occurs in y, and one of the following holds: (i) x is a gene, and x is transcribed into a transcript as part of the gene expression process (ii) x is a transcript, and the transcription of x is part of the gene expression process (iii) x is a mature gene product such as a protein, and x was translated or otherwise processes from a transcript that was transcribed as part of this gene expression process

label: directly develops from
description: Candidate definition: x directly_develops from y if and only if there exists some developmental process (GO:0032502) p such that x and y both participate in p, and x is the output of p and y is the input of p, and a substantial portion of the matter of x comes from y, and the start of x is coincident with or after the end of y.

label: has parasitoid
description: inverse of parasitoid of

label: directly develops into
description: inverse of directly develops from

label: regulates
description: p regulates q iff p is causally upstream of q, the execution of p is not constant and varies according to specific conditions, and p influences the rate or magnitude of execution of q due to an effect either on some enabler of q or some enabler of a part of q.

label: negatively regulates
description: p negatively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p decreases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

label: positively regulates
description: p positively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p increases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

label: has prototype
description: x has prototype y if and only if x is an instance of C and y is a prototypical instance of C. For example, every instance of heart, both normal and abnormal is related by the has prototype relation to some instance of a "canonical" heart, which participates in blood circulation.

label: surrounded by
description: x surrounded_by y if and only if (1) x is adjacent to y and for every region r that is adjacent to x, r overlaps y (2) the shared boundary between x and y occupies the majority of the outermost boundary of x

label: surrounds
description: inverse of surrounded by

label: starts
description: inverse of starts with

label: starts with
description: x starts with y if and only if x has part y and the time point at which x starts is equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: α(y) = α(x) ∧ ω(y) < ω(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

label: develops in
description: x develops_in y if x is located in y whilst x is developing

label: obligate parasite of
description: A sub-relation of parasite-of in which the parasite that cannot complete its life cycle without a host.

label: ends
description: inverse of ends with

label: ends with
description: x ends with y if and only if x has part y and the time point at which x ends is equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: α(y) > α(x) ∧ ω(y) = ω(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

label: has start location
description: x 'has starts location' y if and only if there exists some process z such that x 'starts with' z and z 'occurs in' y

label: has end location
description: x 'has end location' y if and only if there exists some process z such that x 'ends with' z and z 'occurs in' y

label: has input
description: p has input c iff: p is a process, c is a material entity, c is a participant in p, c is present at the start of p, and the state of c is modified during p.

label: has output
description: p has output c iff c is a participant in p, c is present at the end of p, and c is not present in the same state at the beginning of p.

label: has component participant
description: X 'has component participant' Y means X 'has participant' Y and there is a cardinality constraint that specifies the numbers of Ys.

label: has exposure receptor
description: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and any entity (e.g., an organism, organism population, or an organism part) that interacts with an exposure stimulus during the exposure event.

label: has exposure stressor
description: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and any agent, stimulus, activity, or event that causes stress or tension on an organism and interacts with an exposure receptor during an exposure event.

label: has exposure route
description: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and a process by which the exposure stressor comes into contact with the exposure receptor

label: over-expressed in
description: g is over-expressed in t iff g is expressed in t, and the expression level of g is increased relative to some background.

label: under-expressed in
description: g is under-expressed in t iff g is expressed in t, and the expression level of g is decreased relative to some background.

label: has active ingredient
description: A relationship that holds between a substance and a chemical entity, if the chemical entity is part of the substance, and the chemical entity forms the biologically active component of the substance.

label: connecting branch of
description: b connecting-branch-of s iff b is connected to s, and there exists some tree-like structure t such that the mereological sum of b plus s is either the same as t or a branching-part-of t.

label: has connecting branch
description: inverse of connecting branch of

label: developmentally contributes to
description: inverse of has developmental contribution from

label: developmentally induced by
description: t1 induced_by t2 if there is a process of developmental induction (GO:0031128) with t1 and t2 as interacting participants. t2 causes t1 to change its fate from a precursor material anatomical entity type T to T', where T' develops_from T

label: developmentally induces
description: Inverse of developmentally induced by

label: developmentally preceded by
description: Candidate definition: x developmentally related to y if and only if there exists some developmental process (GO:0032502) p such that x and y both participates in p, and x is the output of p and y is the input of p

label: has biological role
description: c has-biological-role r iff c has-role r and r is a biological role (CHEBI:24432)

label: has application role
description: c has-application-role r iff c has-role r and r is an application role (CHEBI:33232)

label: has chemical role
description: c has-chemical-role r iff c has-role r and r is a chemical role (CHEBI:51086)

label: acts upstream of
description: c acts upstream of p if and only if c enables some f that is involved in p' and p' occurs chronologically before p, is not part of p, and affects the execution of p. c is a material entity and f, p, p' are processes.

label: developmentally succeeded by
description: Inverse of developmentally preceded by

label: ubiquitously expressed in
description: x is ubiquitously expressed in y if and only if x is expressed in y, and the majority of cells in y express x

label: expresses
description: y expresses x if and only if there is a gene expression process (GO:0010467) that occurs in y, and one of the following holds: (i) x is a gene, and x is transcribed into a transcript as part of the gene expression process (ii) x is a transcript, and x was transcribed from a gene as part of the gene expression process (iii) x is a mature gene product (protein or RNA), and x was translated or otherwise processed from a transcript that was transcribed as part of the gene expression process.

label: ubiquitously expresses
description: inverse of ubiquiotously expressed in

label: has habitat
description: x 'has habitat' y if and only if: x is an organism, y is a habitat, and y can sustain and allow the growth of a population of xs.

label: has exposure stimulus
description: A relationship between an exposure event or process and any agent, stimulus, activity, or event that causally effects an organism and interacts with an exposure receptor during an exposure event.

label: evolutionarily related to
description: A relationship that holds via some environmental process

label: ecologically related to
description: A relationship that is mediated in some way by the environment or environmental feature (ENVO:00002297)

label: mereotopologically related to
description: A mereological relationship or a topological relationship

label: developmentally related to
description: A relationship that holds between entities participating in some developmental process (GO:0032502)

label: colocalizes with
description: a colocalizes_with b if and only if  a is transiently or peripherally associated with b[GO].

label: enables
description: c enables p iff c is capable of p and c acts to execute p.

label: functionally related to
description: A grouping relationship for any relationship directly involving a function, or that holds because of a function of one of the related entities.

label: genomically related to
description: holds between two entities when some genome-level process such as gene expression is involved. This includes transcriptional, spliceosomal events. These relations can be used between either macromolecule entities (such as regions of nucleic acid) or between their abstract informational counterparts.

label: involved in
description: c involved_in p if and only if c enables some process p', and p' is part of p

label: regulates levels of
description: p regulates levels of c if p regulates some amount (PATO:0000070) of c

label: enabled by
description: inverse of enables

label: regulated by
description: inverse of regulates

label: negatively regulated by
description: inverse of negatively regulates

label: positively regulated by
description: inverse of positively regulates

label: imports
description: Holds between p and c when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c to a destination that is part of some s, where the start location of c is part of the region that surrounds s.

label: exports
description: Holds between p and c when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c to a destination that is part of some s, where the end location of c is part of the region that surrounds s.

label: has member
description: has member is a mereological relation between a collection and an item.

label: attached to
description: a is attached to b if and only if a and b are discrete objects or object parts, and there are physical connections between a and b such that a force pulling a will move b, or a force pulling b will move a

label: has muscle origin
description: m has_muscle_origin s iff m is attached_to s, and it is the case that when m contracts, s does not move. The site of the origin tends to be more proximal and have greater mass than what the other end attaches to.

label: has muscle insertion
description: m has_muscle_insertion s iff m is attaches_to s, and it is the case that when m contracts, s moves. Insertions are usually connections of muscle via tendon to bone.

label: branching part of
description: x is a branching part of y if and only if x is part of y and x is connected directly or indirectly to the main stem of y

label: has direct input
description: p has direct input c iff c is a participant in p, c is present at the start of p, and the state of c is modified during p.

label: indirectly positively regulates
description: p indirectly positively regulates q iff p is indirectly causally upstream of q and p positively regulates q.

label: indirectly negatively regulates
description: p indirectly negatively regulates q iff p is indirectly causally upstream of q and p negatively regulates q.

label: causally related to
description: relation that links two events, processes, states, or objects such that one event, process, state, or object (a cause) contributes to the production of another event, process, state, or object (an effect) where the cause is partly or wholly responsible for the effect, and the effect is partly or wholly dependent on the cause.

label: provides input for
description: p provides input for q iff p is immediately causally upstream of q, and there exists some c such that p has_output c and q has_input c.

label: is active in
description: c executes activity in d if and only if c enables p and p occurs_in d.  Assuming no action at a distance by gene products, if a gene product enables (is capable of) a process that occurs in some structure, it must have at least some part in that structure.

label: interacts with
description: A relationship that holds between two entities in which the processes executed by the two entities are causally connected.

label: molecularly interacts with
description: An interaction relationship in which the two partners are molecular entities that directly physically interact with each other for example via a stable binding interaction or a brief interaction during which one modifies the other.

label: biotically interacts with
description: An interaction relationship in which at least one of the partners is an organism and the other is either an organism or an abiotic entity with which the organism interacts.

label: trophically interacts with
description: An interaction relationship in which the partners are related via a feeding relationship.

label: preys on
description: An interaction relationship involving a predation process, where the subject kills the target in order to eat it or to feed to siblings, offspring or group members

label: symbiotically interacts with
description: A biotic interaction in which the two organisms live together in more or less intimate association.

label: commensually interacts with
description: An interaction relationship between two organisms living together in more or less intimate association in a relationship in which one benefits and the other is unaffected (GO).

label: mutualistically interacts with
description: An interaction relationship between two organisms living together in more or less intimate association in a relationship in which both organisms benefit from each other (GO).

label: parasitized by
description: Inverse of parasite of

label: transmitted by
description: A relationship that holds between a disease and organism

label: has symptom
description: A relation that holds between a disease or an organism and a phenotype

label: has host
description: X 'has host' y if and only if: x is an organism, y is an organism, and x can live on the surface of or within the body of y

label: acquires nutrients from
description: Inverse of provides nutrients for

label: preyed upon by
description: inverse of preys on

label: provides nutrients for
description: A biotic interaction where a material entity provides nutrition for an organism.

label: eats
description: A biotic interaction where one organism consumes a material entity through a type of mouth or other oral opening.

label: is eaten by
description: Inverse of eats

label: is evidence for
description:
A relationship between a piece of evidence a and some entity b, where b is  an information content entity, material entity or process, and 
the a supports either the existence of b, or the truth value of b.

label: composed primarily of
description: x composed_primarily_of y if and only if more than half of the mass of x is made from y or units of the same type as y.

label: ubiquitinates
description: An interaction relation between x and y in which x catalyzes a reaction in which one or more ubiquitin groups are added to y

label: existence starts during
description: x existence starts during y if and only if the time point at which x starts is after or equivalent to the time point at which y starts and before or equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: x existence starts during y iff α(x) >= α(y) & α(x) <= ω(y).

label: existence starts with
description: x starts ends with y if and only if the time point at which x starts is equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: x existence starts with y iff α(x) = α(y).

label: existence overlaps
description: x existence overlaps y if and only if either (a) the start of x is part of y or (b) the end of x is part of y. Formally: x existence starts and ends during y iff (α(x) >= α(y) & α(x) <= ω(y)) OR (ω(x) <= ω(y) & ω(x) >= α(y))

label: existence ends during
description: x existence ends during y if and only if the time point at which x ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which y ends and after or equivalent to the point at which y starts. Formally: x existence ends during y iff ω(x) <= ω(y) and ω(x) >= α(y).

label: existence ends with
description: x existence ends with y if and only if the time point at which x ends is equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: x existence ends with y iff ω(x) = ω(y).

label: has intermediate
description: p has intermediate c if and only if p has parts p1, p2 and p1 has output c, and p2 has input c

label: determined by
description: s determined by f if and only if s is a type of system, and f is a material entity that is part of s, such that f exerts a strong causal influence on the functioning of s, and the removal of f would cause the collapse of s.

label: determines
description: inverse of determined by

label: transcribed from
description: x is transcribed from y if and only if x is synthesized from template y

label: transcribed to
description: inverse of transcribed from

label: ribosomally translates to
description: inverse of ribosomal translation of

label: sequentially related to
description: A relation that holds between two entities that have the property of being sequences or having sequences.

label: has start sequence
description: x has start sequence y if the start of x is identical to the start of y, and x has y as a subsequence

label: has end sequence
description: x has end sequence y if the end of x is identical to the end of y, and x has y as a subsequence

label: bounds sequence of
description: x bounds the sequence of y iff the upstream-most part of x is upstream of or coincident with the upstream-most part of y, and the downstream-most part of x is downstream of or coincident with the downstream-most part of y

label: has subsequence
description: x has subsequence y iff all of the sequence parts of y are sequence parts of x

label: is subsequence of
description: inverse of has subsequence

label: has skeleton
description: A relation between a segment or subdivision of an organism and the maximal subdivision of material entities that provides structural support for that segment or subdivision.

label: hyperparasitized by
description: inverse of hyperparasite of

label: has pathogen
description: A host interaction where the smaller of the two members of a symbiosis causes a disease in the larger member

label: has evidence
description: inverse of is evidence for

label: causally influences
description: The entity or characteristic A is causally upstream of the entity or characteristic B, A having an effect on B. An entity corresponds to any biological type of entity as long as a mass is measurable. A characteristic corresponds to a particular specificity of an entity (e.g., phenotype, shape, size).

label: biomechanically related to
description: A relation that holds between elements of a musculoskeletal system or its analogs.

label: has muscle antagonist
description: m1 has_muscle_antagonist m2 iff m1 has_muscle_insertion s, m2 has_muscle_insection s, m1 acts in opposition to m2, and m2 is responsible for returning the structure to its initial position.

label: has branching part
description: inverse of branching part of

label: has modifier
description: A relation that holds between an attribute or a qualifier and another attribute.

label: directly regulates
description: p directly regulates q iff p is immediately causally upstream of q and p regulates q.

label: capable of regulating
description: Holds between c and p if and only if c is capable of some activity a, and a regulates p.

label: is marker for
description: c is marker for d iff the presence or occurrence of d is correlated with the presence of occurrence of c, and the observation of c is used to infer the presence or occurrence of d. Note that this does not imply that c and d are in a direct causal relationship, as it may be the case that there is a third entity e that stands in a direct causal relationship with c and d.

label: correlated with
description: A relationship that holds between two entities, where the entities exhibit a statistical dependence relationship. The entities may be statistical variables, or they may be other kinds of entities such as diseases, chemical entities or processes.

label: has model
description: Inverse of is-model-of

label: directly positively regulates
description: p directly positively regulates q iff p is immediately causally upstream of q, and p positively regulates q.

label: directly negatively regulates
description: p directly negatively regulates q iff p is immediately causally upstream of q, and p negatively regulates q.

label: has ectoparasite
description: inverse of ectoparasite of

label: has mesoparasite
description: inverse of mesoparasite of

label: has intercellular endoparasite
description: inverse of intercellular endoparasite of

label: has intracellular endoparasite
description: inverse of intracellular endoparasite of

label: co-roosts with
description: Two or more individuals sharing the same roost site (cave, mine, tree or tree hollow, animal burrow, leaf tent, rock crack, space in man-made structure, etc.). Individuals that are sharing a communal roost may be said to be co-roosting. The roost may be either a day roost where the individuals rest during daytime hours, or a night roost where individuals roost to feed, groom, or rest in between flights and/or foraging bouts.  Communal roosting as thus defined is an umbrella term within which different specialized types -- which are not mutually exclusive -- may be recognized based on taxonomy and the temporal and spatial relationships of the individuals that are co-roosting.

label: has reservoir host
description: inverse of reservoir host of

label: produced by
description: a produced_by b iff some process that occurs_in b has_output a.

label: represses expression of
description: Holds between entity A (a transcription factor) and a nucleic acid B if and only if A down-regulates the expression of B. The nucleic acid can be a gene or an mRNA.

label: increases expression of
description: Holds between entity A (a transcription factor) and nucleic acid B if and only if A up-regulates the expression of B. The nucleic acid can be a gene or mRNA.

label: causes condition
description: A relationship between an entity (e.g. a genotype, genetic variation, chemical, or environmental exposure) and a condition (a phenotype or disease), where the entity has some causal role for the condition.

label: contributes to condition
description: A relationship between an entity (e.g. a genotype, genetic variation, chemical, or environmental exposure) and a condition (a phenotype or disease), where the entity has some contributing role that influences the condition.

label: ameliorates condition
description: A relationship between an entity (e.g. a genotype, genetic variation, chemical, or environmental exposure) and a condition (a phenotype or disease), where the presence of the entity reduces or eliminates some or all aspects of the condition.

label: correlated with condition
description: A relationship between an entity and a condition (phenotype or disease) with which it exhibits a statistical dependence relationship.

label: exacerbates condition
description: A relationship between an entity (e.g. a chemical, environmental exposure, or some form of genetic variation) and a condition (a phenotype or disease), where the presence of the entity worsens some or all aspects of the condition.

label: condition ameliorated by
description: A relationship between a condition (a phenotype or disease) and an entity (e.g. a chemical, environmental exposure, or some form of genetic variation) where some or all aspects of the condition are reduced or eliminated by the presence of the entity.

label: condition exacerbated by
description: A relationship between a condition (a phenotype or disease) and an entity (e.g. a chemical, environmental exposure, or some form of genetic variation) where some or all aspects of the condition are worsened by the presence of the entity.

label: has primary output
description: p has primary output c if (a) p has output c and (b) the goal of process is to modify, produce, or transform c.

label: has primary input
description: p has primary input c if (a) p has input c and (b) the goal of process is to modify, consume, or transform c.

label: disease has location
description: A relationship between a disease and an anatomical entity where the disease has one or more features that are located in that entity.

label: disease has feature
description: A relationship between a disease and some feature of that disease, where the feature is either a phenotype or an isolated disease.

label: enables subfunction
description: Holds between an entity and an process P where the entity enables some larger compound process, and that larger process has-part P.

label: has driver
description: A relation between two entities, in which one of the entities is any natural or human-influenced factor that directly or indirectly causes a change in the other entity.

label: has disease driver
description: A relation between an entity and a disease of a host, in which the entity is not part of the host itself, and the condition results in pathological processes.

label: has epiphyte
description: inverse of epiphyte of

label: kleptoparasitized by
description: inverse of kleptoparasite of

label: creates habitat for
description: An interaction relationship wherein one organism creates a structure or environment that is lived in by another organism.

label: ecologically co-occurs with
description: An interaction relationship describing organisms that often occur together at the same time and space or in the same environment.

label: lays eggs on
description: An interaction relationship in which organism a lays eggs on the outside surface of organism b. Organism b is neither helped nor harmed in the process of egg laying or incubation.

label: has roost
description: x 'has roost' y if and only if: x is an organism, y is a habitat, and y can support rest behaviors x.

label: has substance added
description: "has substance added" is a relation existing between a (physical) entity and a substance in which the entity has had the substance added to it at some point in time.

label: has substance removed
description: "has substance removed" is a relation existing between two physical entities in which the first entity has had the second entity (a substance) removed from it at some point in time.

label: immersed in
description: "immersed in" is a relation between a (physical) entity and a fluid substance in which the entity is wholely or substantially surrounded by the substance.

label: has consumer
description: 'has consumer' is a relation between a material entity and an organism in which the former can normally be digested or otherwise absorbed by the latter without immediate or persistent ill effect.

label: assay measures characteristic
description: A relation between an assay and a characteristic, in which the assay generates a data item which is a measure of a characteristic.

label: generically depends on
description: A generically dependent continuant *b* generically depends on an independent continuant *c* at time *t* means: there inheres in *c* a specifically deendent continuant which concretizes *b* at *t*.

label: is carrier of
description: *b* is carrier of *c* at time *t* if and only if *c* *g-depends on* *b* at *t*

label: regulates activity of
description: The entity A has an activity that regulates an activity of the entity B. For example, A and B are gene products where the catalytic activity of A regulates the kinase activity of B.

label: regulates quantity of
description: The entity A has an activity that regulates the quantity or abundance or concentration of the entity B.

label: destabilizes quantity of
description: An entity A directly interacts with B and A has an activity that decreases the amount of an entity B by degradating it.

label: stabilizes quantity of
description: An entity A physically interacts with B and A has an activity that increases the amount of an entity B by stabilizing it.

label: removes input for
description: p removes input for q iff p is causally upstream of q, there exists some c such that p has_input c and q has_input c, p reduces the levels of c, and c is rate limiting for execution of q.

label: indirectly regulates
description: p indirectly regulates q iff p is indirectly causally upstream of q and p regulates q.

label: has exemplar data
description: A relation between a material entity and some data in which the data is taken as exemplifying the material entity.

label: has cross section
description: s3 has_cross_section s3 if and only if : there exists some 2d plane that intersects the bearer of s3, and the impression of s3 upon that plane has shape quality s2.

label: has disease
description: A relationship that holds between an organism and a disease. Here a disease is construed broadly as a disposition to undergo pathological processes that exists in an organism because of one or more disorders in that organism.

label: has exposure medium
description: X has exposure medium Y if X is an exposure event (process), Y is a material entity, and the stimulus for X is transmitted or carried in Y.

label: device utilizes material
description: X device utilizes material Y means X and Y are material entities, and X is capable of some process P that has input Y.

label: positively correlated with
description: A relation between entities in which one increases or decreases as the other does the same.

label: negatively correlated with
description: A relation between entities in which one increases as the other decreases.

label: contains measured amount
description: A relation between a container and measurement datum that specifies the actual amount of material in the container.

label: has maximum capacity
description: A relation that relates a container to a measurement datum that specifies the maximum capacity of the container. Capacity can refer to either weight or volume.

label: owns
description: A primitive relation that holds between entities x and y in which y is at x's full disposal.

label: is owned by
description: Inverse of the owns relation.

label: is myristoyltransferase activity
description: Helper relation for OWL definition of RO:0018002 myristoylates

label: myristoylated by
description: inverse of myristoylates

label: is agonist of
description: a relation between a ligand (material entity) and a receptor (material entity) that implies the binding of the ligand to the receptor activates some activity of the receptor

label: is antagonist of
description: a relation between a ligand (material entity) and a receptor (material entity) that implies the binding of the ligand to the receptor reduces some activity of the receptor to basal level

label: is tautomer of
description:
Two chemicals are tautomers if they can be readily interconverted.

This commonly refers to prototropy in which a hydrogen's position is changed, such as between ketones and enols. This is also often observed in heterocyclic rings, e.g., ones containing nitrogens and/or have aryl functional groups containing heteroatoms.

label: has functional parent
description:
Chemical A has functional parent Chemical B if there is chemical transformation through which chemical B can be produced from chemical A. 

For example, the relationship between a salt and a freebased compound is a "has functional parent" relationship.

label: is enantiomer of
description:
Chemicals A and B are enantiomers if they share the same molecular graph except the change of the configuration of substituents around exactly one chiral center.

A chemical with no chiral centers can not have an enantiomer. A chemical with multiple chiral centers can have multiple enantiomers, but its enantiomers are not themselves enantiomers (they are diastereomers).

label: has parent hydride
description: Chemical A has parent hydride Chemical B if there exists a molecular graphical transformation where functional groups on A are replaced with hydrogens in order to yield B.

label: regulates characteristic
description: A relationship that holds between a process and a characteristic in which process (P) regulates characteristic (C) iff: P results in the existence of C OR affects the intensity or magnitude of C.

label: positively regulates characteristic
description: A relationship that holds between a process and a characteristic in which process (P) positively regulates characteristic (C) iff: P results in an increase in the intensity or magnitude of C.

label: negatively regulates characteristic
description: A relationship that holds between a process and a characteristic in which process (P) negatively regulates characteristic (C) iff: P results in a decrease in the intensity or magnitude of C.

label: has numerator
description: A relationship between a ratio or proportion and its dividend.

label: has denominator
description: A relationship between a ratio or proportion and its divisor.
